title: Identical expressions should be avoided on both sides of a binary operator
description: Identical expressions should not be used on both sides of a binary operatorAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoUsing the same value on both sides of a binary operator is a code defect. In the case of logical operators, it is either a copy/paste error and, therefore, a bug, or it is simply duplicated code and should be simplified. In the case of bitwise operators and most binary mathematical operators, having the same value on both sides of an operator yields predictable results and should be simplified as well.Noncompliant code exampleif (a == b && a == b) { // if the first one is true, the second one is too doX(); } if (a > a) { // always false doW(); } var j = 5 / 5; //always 1 var k = 5 - 5; //always 0ExceptionsThe specific case of testing
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Identical expressions on both sides of operator - this may be a copy-paste error or redundant code
Identical numeric values in binary operation - consider simplifying (e.g., x/x = 1, x-x = 0)
Variable compared with itself - this is always true/false and likely indicates a logic error
Variable compared with itself using relational operator - this is always false and indicates a bug
